name: unreal-angelscript-tool-calling-fork
description: Language Server and Debug Adapter for the UnrealEngine-Angelscript plugin. Based on https://github.com/jiangxiaoxu/vscode-unreal-angelscript-tool-calling (forked from https://github.com/Hazelight/UnrealEngine-Angelscript).

languageModelTools.name: angelscript_searchApi
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Use when you need to discover Angelscript API symbols before you know the exact symbol name. Do not use when you already have a concrete file position and need symbol resolution. Requires query. Default plain search is case-insensitive, favors code-like queries such as `Type.Member` and `Namespace::Func`, supports ordered token gaps, and uses weak token reorder only as a low-priority fallback. A trailing `(` or `()` means callable-only and restricts plain results to `method` and `function`; it does not match argument lists or zero-arg signatures. Optional controls: limit (default 20, max 200), source (native|script|both, default both), scope, includeInheritedFromScope, includeDocs, and regex. `scope` narrows the search domain to a known namespace or containing type before ranking. When a namespace and type share the same exact scope name, search automatically merges both scopes and may return grouped `scopeGroups` plus aggregate `matchCounts`. When `includeInheritedFromScope` is omitted, class scopes auto-expand inherited methods and properties while other scopes stay off silently; explicit `true` keeps legacy ignored-scope reporting. `includeDocs=true` only enriches returned matches with full documentation and does not affect ranking. When `regex=true`, `query` must use VS Code-style `/pattern/flags` syntax and matches only symbol name views: short names, qualified names, mixin member-view aliases, and callable `...()` views for methods/functions. Structured matches may include `matchReason` to help the model judge confidence. Returns readable code-first text by default; structured JSON is included only when output mode is `text+structured`. Success text groups matches by owner or namespace and renders declarations with minimal comment metadata.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Search Angelscript API symbols with plain name search by default. Use `Type.Member`, `Namespace::Func`, token gaps like `Status AI`, a trailing `(` for callable-only, optional `scope`, or `regex=true` with `/pattern/flags` when needed. Same-name namespace and type scopes merge automatically, and omitted `includeInheritedFromScope` auto-expands class scopes only. `includeDocs=true` only enriches returned matches.

query.description: Search query for Angelscript API symbols. Plain search is case-insensitive and supports code-like queries such as `Type.Member` and `Namespace::Func`, ordered token gaps such as `Status AI`, and a low-priority weak reorder fallback. A trailing `(` or `()` limits plain results to callable symbols (`method` and `function`) without matching argument lists. When `regex=true`, provide a VS Code-style `/pattern/flags` regex.

limit.description: Optional. Maximum number of matches to return. Default is 20.

source.description: Optional. Filter results by source. Supported values: native, script, both. Default is both.

scope.description: Optional. Known namespace or containing type. Type scopes can expand inherited methods and properties, and also surface applicable mixin functions. When a namespace and type share the same exact name, search automatically merges both scopes.

includeInheritedFromScope.description: Optional. Only applies to class/type scopes. When omitted, class scopes auto-expand inherited methods and properties while namespace/struct/enum scopes keep inheritance expansion off. Explicit `true` preserves ignored-scope reporting for invalid or non-class scopes.

includeDocs.description: Optional. Attach full documentation text to returned matches. This enriches results only and does not affect search ranking.

regex.description: Optional. Interpret `query` as a VS Code-style `/pattern/flags` regex. Regex search matches only symbol name views, plus callable `...()` views for methods and functions.

languageModelTools.name: angelscript_getTypeMembers
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Use when you need the member list of a specific Angelscript type, including inherited members when requested. Do not use when you need parent/child hierarchy traversal between classes. Requires exact name (optional namespace for disambiguation). Optional switches: includeInherited (default false), includeDocs (default false), and kinds (both|method|property, default both). Returns readable text by default; structured JSON is included only when output mode is `text+structured`. Structured payload includes `type.name`, `type.namespace`, `type.qualifiedName`, always-on `type.description`, and member entries whose descriptions are populated only when `includeDocs=true`.
languageModelTools.userDescription: List members for a given Angelscript type. Structured JSON is optional via `text+structured` mode.

namespace.description: Optional namespace to disambiguate type name. Use empty string for root namespace.

includeInherited.description: Optional. Include inherited members. Default is false.

includeDocs.description: Optional. Include description text. Default is false.

kinds.description: Optional. Filter by member kind. Default is both.

languageModelTools.name: angelscript_resolveSymbolAtPosition
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Use when you have an absolute file path and cursor position and need to identify the symbol, signature, documentation, or definition. Do not use when your primary goal is collecting all references across the project. Requires absolute filePath and position (line, character, both 1-based). Optional includeDocumentation controls doc payload (default true). Returns readable code-first text by default; structured JSON is included only when output mode is `text+structured`. Success text prefers normalized `/** ... */` docs plus declaration or definition preview. Preview still checks one line above definition start for Unreal reflection macros (UCLASS|UPROPERTY|UFUNCTION|UENUM).
languageModelTools.userDescription: Resolve the symbol at a file position with code-like text. Structured JSON is optional via `text+structured` mode.

filePath.description: Absolute path to the file containing the symbol.

includeDocumentation.description: Include documentation when available. Default is true.

languageModelTools.name: angelscript_getClassHierarchy
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Use when you need class inheritance structure, including parent chain and derived-class expansion. Do not use when you only need members of a single type. Requires exact class name (e.g., "APawn"). Optional limits: maxSuperDepth (default 3), maxSubDepth (default 2), maxSubBreadth (default 10). Returns readable code-first text by default; structured JSON is included only when output mode is `text+structured`. Structured payload includes root, supers, derivedByParent, limits, truncated info, and script-class preview text when available. Success text uses comment-based lineage/derived trees plus source previews or native declaration stubs, and unresolved script sources degrade to `// source unavailable` instead of failing the tool.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Get class hierarchy with code-like lineage comments and previews. Structured JSON is optional via `text+structured` mode.

maxSuperDepth.description: Optional. Maximum number of supertypes to return. Non-negative integer. Default is 3.

maxSubDepth.description: Optional. Maximum depth for subtype tree. Non-negative integer. Default is 2.

maxSubBreadth.description: Optional. Maximum number of direct children returned per class. Non-negative integer. Default is 10. Truncation per class is reported in truncated.derivedBreadthByClass.

languageModelTools.name: angelscript_findReferences
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Use when you have a symbol location and need project references to that symbol. Do not use when you only need to identify what symbol is at the current position. Requires absolute filePath and position (line, character, both 1-based). Optional limit controls the maximum number of returned references (default 30, max 200). Returns readable code-first text by default; structured JSON is included only when output mode is `text+structured`. Structured payload includes total, returned, limit, truncated, per-file grouping, 1-based range labels, and preview text. Success text renders `// <filePath>` + `// range: ...` + preview blocks, with truncation surfaced as a final comment only when needed.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Find symbol references with file-grouped preview blocks. Structured JSON is optional via `text+structured` mode.

filePath.description: Absolute path to the file containing the symbol.

limit.description: Optional. Maximum number of references to return. Default is 30.

UnrealAngelscript.insertParenthesisOnFun.description: Insert parenthesis pair when auto-completing a function call.

UnrealAngelscript.diagnosticsForUnrealNa.description: Emit diagnostic warnings and hints when types, functions, or variables violate the Unreal naming convention.

UnrealAngelscript.markUnreadVariablesAsU.description: Variables that are written to but never read are marked as unused as well.

UnrealAngelscript.mathCompletionShortcut.description: Add completions for functions in the Math:: namespace so they can be completed without typing Math:: first.

UnrealAngelscript.correctFloatLiteralsWh.markdownDescription: When a float literal is typed (eg `1.f`) in a context where double-precision is expected, automatically correct it (eg to `1.0`)

UnrealAngelscript.unrealConnectionPort.description: What port to use when connecting to the Unreal engine game or editor instance for auto-completion and debugging. Must match the debug port configured in the Unreal Engine project settings, or as a launch argument (-asdebugport=).

UnrealAngelscript.languageModelTools.out.description: Controls whether Language Model Tools return readable text only or readable text plus structured JSON payload. Default is text-only.

UnrealAngelscript.inlayHints.inlayHintsE.description: Enable inlay hints rendered by the angelscript extension.

UnrealAngelscript.inlayHints.parameterHi.description: Show parameter name hints when passing a constant literal argument.
Show parameter name hints when passing a complex expression as an argument.

UnrealAngelscript.inlayHints.parameterRe.description: Show an inlay hint when a parameter takes a writeable reference.

UnrealAngelscript.inlayHints.parameterHi.description: Enable parameter name hints for functions that are only passed a single argument.

UnrealAngelscript.inlayHints.typeHintsFo.description: Show the name of the type as an inlay hint on auto declarations.
Types in this list will never receive a type hint for an auto declaration

UnrealAngelscript.inlayHints.parameterHi.description: Parameters with a name included in this list will be ignored for inlay hints.
Functions with a name included in this list will be ignored for inlay hints

UnrealAngelscript.inlineValues.showInlin.description: When debugging, show an inline value next to local variable declarations.
When debugging, show an inline value for function parameters.
When debugging, show an inline value next to direct assignments of this member variables.
When debugging, show an inline value above the function declaration to display the this pointer and Owner of the object.

UnrealAngelscript.scriptIgnorePatterns.description: Glob patterns to ignore when searching for script files to parse.

UnrealAngelscript.codeLenses.showCreateB.description: Script classes inheriting from the specified classes will have a 'Create Blueprint' prompt above them if they don't already have a blueprint implementation.

UnrealAngelscript.dataBreakpoints.cppBre.description: Should Data Breakpoints trigger in C++? If not, any Data Breakpoint will surface as an AS breakpoint, potentially missing the C++ callstack.
How many times a C++ Data Breakpoint should trigger (silently) before activating and disabling, -1 means it will never disable automatically.

UnrealAngelscript.dataBreakpoints.asBrea.description: How many times a AS Data Breakpoint should trigger (silently) before activating and disabling, -1 means it will never disable automatically.

UnrealAngelscript.projectCodeGeneration..description: Should project code generation be enabled? If not, only built-in code generation will be applied.
What generators to apply during analysis.

items.description: A code generator to apply to a given class/struct.

derivedFrom.description: The class this generator will apply to.

staticFunctions.description: List of static functions to generate for the target class.

returnType.description: The type name of the class/struct to be returned. Supports patterns such as `{class}`.

name.description: Name of this function. Supports patterns such as `{class}`.

args.description: List of argument types for this function.

type.description: The type name of the class/struct to be used. Supports patterns such as `{class}`.

name.description: The name of the argument. Supports patterns such as `{class}`.

memberFunctions.description: List of member functions to generate for the target class.

returnType.description: The type name of the class/struct to be returned. Supports patterns such as `{class}`.

name.description: Name of this function. Supports patterns such as `{class}`.

args.description: List of argument types for this function.

type.description: The type name of the class/struct to be used. Supports patterns such as `{class}`.

name.description: The name of the argument. Supports patterns such as `{class}`.

const.description: Whether or not this function is const qualified.

property.description: Whether or not this function marked as a property accessor.

staticAccessors.description: List of static accessors to generate for the target class.

derivedFrom.description: The property type this generator will apply to.

returnType.description: The type name of the class/struct to be returned. Supports patterns such as `{class}, {propType}, {propName}`.

name.description: Name of this function. Supports patterns such as `{class}, {propType}, {propName}`.

args.description: List of argument types for this function.

type.description: The type name of the class/struct to be used. Supports patterns such as `{class}, {propType}, {propName}`.

name.description: The name of the argument. Supports patterns such as `{class}, {propType}, {propName}`.

memberAccessors.description: List of member accessors to generate for the target class.

derivedFrom.description: The property type this generator will apply to.

returnType.description: The type name of the class/struct to be returned. Supports patterns such as `{class}, {propType}, {propName}`.

name.description: Name of this function. Supports patterns such as `{class}, {propType}, {propName}`.

args.description: List of argument types for this function.

type.description: The type name of the class/struct to be used. Supports patterns such as `{class}, {propType}, {propName}`.

name.description: The name of the argument. Supports patterns such as `{class}, {propType}, {propName}`.

const.description: Whether or not this function is const qualified.

property.description: Whether or not this function marked as a property accessor.

UnrealAngelscript.completion.dependencyR.description: Restrictions to apply for which modules can depend on symbols from which other modules

isolate.description: Isolate symbols in modules matching this, so they cannot be used outside that match

unisolate.description: Modules matching this don't obey isolation restrictions

trace.description: Enable logging of the Debug Adapter Protocol.

port.description: Port to AS debug server. If 0, we use the Unreal Connection Port from the extension settings.

commands.title: Unreal: Resume Execution and Stop Any Active PIE Sessions

scripts.compile: npm run compile:extension && npm run compile:language-server
scripts.clean:dist: node -e "require('fs').rmSync('extension/dist',{ recursive: true, force: true }); require('fs').rmSync('language-server/dist',{ recursive: true, force: true });"
scripts.clean:tsc-out: node -e "require('fs').rmSync('extension/out',{ recursive: true, force: true }); require('fs').rmSync('language-server/out',{ recursive: true, force: true });"
scripts.test:fork-boundary: npm run compile && npm run clean:tsc-out && npm exec -- tsc -p extension/tsconfig.json && npm exec -- tsc -p language-server/tsconfig.json && node --test extension/out/__tests__/toolContractUtils.test.js extension/out/__tests__/toolResultTransport.test.js extension/out/__tests__/toolTextFormatter.test.js language-server/out/__tests__/apiSearch.test.js language-server/out/__tests__/getTypeMembers.test.js language-server/out/__tests__/symbolResolve.test.js language-server/out/__tests__/workspaceLayout.test.js
scripts.test: npm run compile && npm run clean:tsc-out && npm exec -- tsc -p extension/tsconfig.json && npm exec -- tsc -p language-server/tsconfig.json && node --test extension/out/__tests__/*.test.js language-server/out/__tests__/*.test.js
scripts.postinstall: cd extension && npm install && cd ../language-server && npm install && cd ..
scripts.pegjs:test: cd language-server/pegjs && peggy angelscript.pegjs --allowed-start-rules start,start_global,start_class,start_enum --trace --test-file test.as -S start
